<!-- Peblla Marketing Team Knowledge Base — Auto-generated from group chat learnings -->

# Video Production Workflow

## Default Production Flow

Applies to all requests (except 紫涵's own — see below).

```
Rex (plan + script)
  → Devil's Advocate challenge → Pass
    → Coco (character design + keyframes + video prompts)
      → Devil's Advocate challenge → Pass
        → Safer review
          → Ace (editing + effects)
            → Devil's Advocate challenge → Pass
              → Safer review
                → Rex (content review)
                  → Discuss with Nana (publishing strategy)
                    → Post to group chat for requester confirmation
```

Each step passes Devil's Advocate QA first, then proceeds. Final delivery goes to **whoever made the request**.

### 刘紫涵's Role

- Trains Rex/Coco/Ace on capabilities and process standards
- After training, the three operate independently for all team members
- Other people's requests do **not** require 紫涵's review

## 紫涵 Accelerated Workflow (Permanent — 2026-03-25)

**Only for 紫涵's own requests.**

```
紫涵 gives theme/creative direction
  → Rex writes proposal
    → 紫涵 confirms direction OK
      → Rex directly notifies Coco to generate full keyframe set + UI + kanban link
        (no segment-by-segment confirmation)
          → 紫涵 reviews images, gives feedback
            → Rex revises proposal/prompts + Coco revises images
              (proposal and kanban updated simultaneously)
                → Polish completed same day
```

**Key difference**: 紫涵 only intervenes at two points — direction confirmation and final image review. No step-by-step approval in between.

## Coco Keyframe Workflow (Permanent — 刘紫涵)

1. Before each shot, check required assets (character / props / UI / scene)
2. If UI needed → generate UI design draft first
3. If character appears → **create scene without character first**, confirm scene, then composite
4. If **new character** → create multi-view reference sheet (full body + half body, multiple angles), confirm before use
5. Existing characters use previously confirmed reference sheets for consistency
6. Each generated image posted to group for confirmation before proceeding
7. Jimeng video generation handled manually by 紫涵; Coco only generates images

## Coco Methodology (Permanent — 2026-03-23)

### Global Read Before Action
Read Rex's entire proposal (story arc, emotional beats, visual style, transitions) before generating any image. Never work from a single shot's prompt in isolation.

### Theme Consistency
All scene images must maintain the video's overall theme. No mixing unrelated business types.

### Divergent Thinking
Within Rex's framework, expand creatively — produce extra reference images as asset reserves.

### Natural Character Poses
Characters must look natural (e.g., one-hand POS operation, not unnatural dual-hand).

### Batch Delivery via Kanban
Package large keyframe batches into a kanban page (HTML dark theme, grouped by ACT), upload to hosting with a single link.

### UI Screenshots as Video Replacement
High-quality UI design drafts can serve as quick-cut images in editing. Also produce "magnifier images" — UI modules popped out and enlarged.

### Keyframe Style (Permanent — 紫涵)
- Realistic photography / on-location shooting style
- Not CG or animation
- Prompt inclusion: "realistic photography style, not CG or animation"

## Ace Methodology (Permanent — 2026-03-23)

### Editing is NOT a Slideshow
No large blank areas, no simple slide-up-bounce, no basic effects. Maintain SaaS ad film-level polish.

### No Cheap Decorations
No orange bars, colorful borders, or decorative elements. Clean and premium.

### Dual Images Must Coexist
Two images must appear on the same frame (diagonal layout + 3D perspective). Never full-screen alternation.

### Text-Image Association
Text animation must connect visually with images. No isolated floating text.

### UI Module Animation
Extract **complete cards** (gradient + title + tags + buttons) from UI screenshots, not just color blocks. Popped-out elements must be readable.

### Dynamic Effects (Baseline)
- Elastic easing (ease-out-back)
- Staggered timing
- Breathing float after landing
- Drop shadow + rounded corners

## Ace Capability Boundary (Permanent — 2026-03-24)

| Can Do | Cannot Do |
|--------|-----------|
| Edit individual shots | Full end-to-end video editing pipeline |
| Splice two shots with effects + text | Replace human editor for complete videos |
| Simple AE-style effects | Complex multi-track compositing |
| Emergency single-shot work | Sustained high-volume output |

Position: Like an AI video generation tool — can assist and handle simple work, but requires human collaboration for complete delivery.
